name: Teachers
description_short: Community & Teachers Federal Credit Union is a Rhode Island-based, member-owned credit union serving employees and residents across multiple counties with full-service banking and lending.
description_long:
Community & Teachers Federal Credit Union was founded in 1960 by eight teachers in Rhode Island and has operated as a not-for-profit cooperative financial institution for over 55 years. Originally restricted to school department employees, the credit union expanded under a Community charter to serve anyone who lives, works, worships, or attends school in select Rhode Island counties, as well as their family members.

The credit union offers comprehensive financial services including share accounts, share certificates, personal loans, mortgages, and various savings products. Members can access accounts through online banking, mobile platforms, ATMs, drive-up windows, and in-person appointments. The institution provides financial education through one-on-one consultations, mortgage assistance, and credit report guidance to help members understand their finances and navigate major financial decisions.

Community & Teachers distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community involvement and member education. The credit union actively supports local charities, community events, youth and sports programs, and educational initiatives. As a credit union rather than a for-profit bank, it operates on a cooperative model where members are owners, and profits are returned to members through better rates and lower fees.

The credit union has limited physical presence with appointment-only lobby access and drive-up hours Monday-Saturday. Membership eligibility is restricted to specific geographic areas and employment/community connections in Rhode Island, which limits accessibility for those outside its service territory. The website contains some irrelevant content (McDonald's reference) and lacks detailed rate or product information, making it difficult to compare offerings with other financial institutions.
services: Personal loans with rates posted online
Online banking and mobile app access
pros: Not-for-profit credit union structure returns profits to members through better rates and lower fees
Established 55+ year history serving Rhode Island community with strong local ties
Comprehensive services including mortgages, personal loans, share accounts, and online banking
One-on-one financial consultations and education provided by credit union representatives
Active community involvement supporting local charities, youth programs, and educational initiatives
Drive-up window access available Monday-Saturday for convenient teller transactions
Free online banking and mobile services for account management and check deposits
cons: Limited to Rhode Island-based service area with restricted membership eligibility, excluding most consumers
Appointment-only lobby access significantly restricts in-person accessibility compared to traditional banks
Minimal product and rate transparency on website; no specific APR, fee, or loan product details disclosed
Limited physical branch presence compared to larger regional or national financial institutions
Coin transaction services only available by appointment, creating friction for common banking needs
best_for: Rhode Island residents or employees who qualify for membership and seek a community-focused financial institution
Teachers and education professionals, given the credit union's historical mission and founding
Community members seeking personalized financial guidance and one-on-one loan consultation
Consumers prioritizing community involvement and supporting local charitable causes through their banking
diagnosis: Community & Teachers Federal Credit Union is best for Rhode Island residents and employees who qualify for membership and want a cooperative, community-focused financial institution with personalized service. The main caveat is severely limited geographic eligibility and minimal physical branch access; most consumers outside Rhode Island cannot join, and even qualified members must schedule appointments for most in-person banking.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Teachers — Credit Union in Smithtown, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Teachers — NCUA-insured credit union in Smithtown, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
